# Requirements Quality Checklist: Log In Use Case

**Purpose**: Validate completeness, clarity, and consistency of the log in requirements.
**Created**: 2026-02-09
**Feature**: /home/aiden/ECE_493_Lab_2/specs/002-log-in/spec.md

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all inputs required for log in explicitly listed (e.g., username/email and password)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK002 Are requirements defined for how invalid credentials are detected and reported? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements specified for account locked/disabled handling beyond a single scenario mention? [Gap, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios]
- [ ] CHK004 Are requirements included for unavailable authentication/storage services? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK005 Are requirements specified for session creation, duration, and termination? [Gap]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is “log in” defined with explicit success outcomes (session established, role-appropriate home page)? [Clarity, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios]
- [ ] CHK007 Is “clear, actionable error message” defined with minimum content expectations? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK008 Is “No session created on failure” defined with measurable criteria (e.g., no auth token/state set)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK009 Do acceptance scenarios align with the functional requirements for validation and error handling? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK010 Do edge cases map to explicit requirements rather than remaining open questions? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK011 Are success criteria measurable without relying on implementation details? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK012 Are success criteria aligned with the defined scope (single-user demo vs. general user base)? [Consistency, Spec §SC-001, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK013 Are acceptance scenarios complete for all failure types listed (invalid credentials, locked/disabled, service unavailable)? [Coverage, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK014 Are primary and exception flows fully specified for log in? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [ ] CHK015 Are recovery actions defined after a failed log in attempt? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 2]
- [ ] CHK016 Are retry or lockout behaviors defined for repeated failures? [Gap]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK017 Are edge cases written as concrete requirements rather than open questions? [Ambiguity, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK018 Are boundary conditions defined for credential validation (format, length)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK019 Are performance expectations specified in the requirements (not just the plan)? [Gap]
- [ ] CHK020 Are accessibility requirements defined for the log in form and errors? [Gap]
- [ ] CHK021 Are privacy or data handling requirements specified for credentials? [Gap]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK022 Are assumptions about account status rules (locked/disabled) explicitly tracked with a resolution path? [Assumption, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios]
- [ ] CHK023 Are dependencies on external artifacts (use cases, scenarios, acceptance tests) explicitly mapped? [Traceability, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK024 Are role-based landing page requirements unambiguous and consistent across scenarios and requirements? [Ambiguity, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios]
- [ ] CHK025 Are there any conflicts between acceptance scenarios and functional requirements about success outcomes? [Conflict, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios, Spec §FR-001]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
